footer.tagline: Engineering intelligent digital systems for ambitious organisations.
footer.servingLine: Serving clients in the United Kingdom and internationally.

hero.headline: Engineering intelligent digital systems for ambitious organisations.
hero.subhead: We design AI solutions, software platforms and secure digital infrastructure for businesses in the United Kingdom and worldwide.

narrative.step1Text: A distributed system of clients, data and infrastructure — connected, wherever they are.
narrative.step2Text: That network becomes structure: modular systems built to be understood, extended, and trusted.
narrative.step3Text: Structure becomes a working interface — dashboards, automation and AI agents operating on real data.
narrative.step4Text: Underneath it all, infrastructure engineered for resilience, security and scale.

capabilities.heading: Twelve disciplines, one accountable team.
capabilities.sub: From first architecture decision to production support — nothing handed off, nothing outsourced.

why.heading: Built for organisations that can't afford to get this wrong.
why.point1Text: We work to the standard expected by UK and international clients — clear scope, clear timelines, clear accountability.
why.point2Text: Authentication, encryption and access control are architectural decisions, not features added at the end.
why.point3Text: Clean, documented architecture handed over in full — no hidden dependency on us to keep it running.
why.point4Text: Design, engineering, AI and security under one roof — not five vendors you have to coordinate yourself.

cta.heading: Let's talk about your project.
cta.sub: Clear proposal within 48 hours. No obligation on the first conversation.

hero.headline: Twelve disciplines. One team that ships.
hero.sub: Select a discipline to see how we approach it — what it solves, how we deliver it, and the technology behind it.

process.heading: The same process, whichever discipline you need.

steps.title: Discover
steps.desc: We start with your problem, not our tech stack — scope, constraints, and what success actually looks like.

steps.title: Design
steps.desc: Architecture and interface decisions made explicit before a line of production code is written.

steps.title: Build
steps.desc: Iterative delivery with visible progress — you see working software early, not just at the end.

steps.title: Support
steps.desc: Handover with full documentation, and continuity after launch if you need it.

items.name: Artificial Intelligence
items.tagline: AI systems built around your data
items.description: We design and build applied AI systems — models, pipelines and integrations — grounded in your actual data and workflows, not a generic off-the-shelf model wrapped in a chat window.
benefits: Decisions and outputs grounded in your real data, not generic training
Clear boundaries on what the system can and can't do, agreed upfront
Built to integrate with the systems you already run
problems: Manual work that follows a repeatable pattern but hasn't been automated
Data that exists but isn't being used to inform decisions
Generic AI tools that don't understand your specific domain
items.example: A support team drowning in repetitive tickets could use a retrieval-augmented system trained on their own documentation to draft first-response answers for a human to review — cutting response time without removing human judgement from the loop.

items.name: AI Agents and Automation
items.description: Agents that carry out real, multi-step processes — checking data, taking actions across systems, and flagging what needs a human — rather than a single chatbot reply.
benefits: Removes repetitive multi-step work from your team's plate
Human-in-the-loop checkpoints for anything with real consequences
Auditable — every action an agent takes is logged and traceable
problems: Staff time spent on multi-step processes that follow clear rules
Delays caused by manual handoffs between systems or people
Errors from manual data entry between disconnected tools
items.example: A business that manually re-keys data between a booking form and its invoicing system could have an agent do the transfer automatically, validate it against business rules, and only escalate to a person when something looks wrong.

items.name: Custom Software Development
items.tagline: Software built for your exact requirements
items.description: Bespoke, business-critical software engineered around how your organisation actually operates, rather than a generic product bent to fit.
benefits: No paying for features you don't need or working around ones you do
Built to scale with your business rather than needing a rebuild
You own the codebase and the architecture decisions behind it
problems: Off-the-shelf software that covers 80% of a need and fights you on the rest
Processes that live in spreadsheets because no tool fits them
Legacy systems that are too fragile to extend safely
items.example: An operation running critical processes across three disconnected spreadsheets could have a single purpose-built system that reflects how the business actually works, with proper access control and an audit trail.

items.name: Web Platforms
items.description: Web platforms engineered for speed, reliability and growth — from marketing sites that convert to complex web applications that run your operation.
benefits: Fast by default — performance treated as a requirement, not an afterthought
Built to handle real traffic and real usage growth
SEO and accessibility considered from the first architecture decision
problems: A site that's slow, hard to update, or breaks under real usage
A platform that can't be extended without a rebuild
Poor search visibility from technical debt baked into the foundations
items.example: A growing business outgrowing a template-based website could move to a platform that's genuinely fast, correctly structured for search, and built to add new capability without starting over.

items.name: Mobile Applications
items.description: iOS and Android applications — native or cross-platform depending on what the product actually needs — connected to the same systems as the rest of your platform.
benefits: Platform choice driven by your product's needs, not by default habit
Connected to your existing backend and data, not a silo
Built with app store review and release process in mind from day one
problems: Customers expecting a mobile app where you currently offer none
An existing app that's slow, unreliable, or hard to update
Field or frontline staff needing mobile access to systems that are currently desktop-only
items.example: A service business whose staff currently fill in paper forms in the field could get a mobile app that captures the same information digitally and syncs it straight into the back office.

items.name: SaaS Products
items.description: SaaS products architected from the outset for multiple customers, subscription billing, roles and permissions — the infrastructure a real product needs, not a single-customer app relabelled.
benefits: Multi-tenant architecture that scales to your next customer, not just your first
Subscription and billing logic built in from the start
Role-based access so each customer only sees what they should
problems: An idea validated with one customer that now needs to serve many
A product that needs proper account, team and billing structures
Uncertainty about how to architect for multiple tenants safely
items.example: A tool built to solve one company's internal problem could be re-architected into a proper multi-tenant SaaS product, with each customer's data correctly isolated and its own billing plan.

items.name: Cloud Infrastructure
items.description: Cloud infrastructure designed for resilience and scale, with automated deployment pipelines so releases are routine, not risky.
benefits: Infrastructure that scales with demand instead of falling over under it
Continuous deployment — releases become low-risk and frequent
Monitoring and alerting so problems are caught before customers notice
problems: Downtime during releases or traffic spikes
Manual, error-prone deployment processes
No visibility into system health until something breaks
items.example: A platform that currently requires a manual, out-of-hours deployment process could move to automated, zero-downtime deployments with monitoring that flags issues before customers ever see them.

items.name: Cybersecurity
items.tagline: Security designed in, not bolted on
items.description: Authentication, encryption and access control treated as architecture decisions from the start — the practical security work that protects a real system in production.
benefits: Strong authentication and access control as a foundation, not an add-on
Data encrypted in transit and at rest by default
Access logging so you know who did what, and when
problems: Systems handling sensitive data without proper access control
No clear answer to 'who can see this, and why'
Security treated as a checklist item after launch rather than a design input
items.example: A platform storing customer records without row-level access control could have that control retrofitted so each customer's data is strictly isolated from every other customer's, with every access logged.

items.name: Data and Analytics
items.description: Turning the data your systems already generate into dashboards and reports your team can actually act on, rather than raw numbers nobody has time to interpret.
benefits: Decisions based on what's actually happening, not what people assume is happening
Dashboards built around the questions your team actually asks
Data pipelines that stay accurate as your systems change
problems: Data spread across systems with no single accurate view
Reports that take days to compile manually
No reliable way to answer 'how is the business actually performing?'
items.example: A business making decisions from a monthly manually-assembled spreadsheet could move to a live dashboard pulling directly from its operational systems, cutting reporting time from days to real time.

items.name: UI/UX Product Design
items.description: Product design grounded in how the people who actually use your software work — not visual trends borrowed from unrelated products.
benefits: Interfaces designed around real user tasks, not aesthetic trends
Consistent design system that speeds up every future feature
Accessibility considered as a requirement, not an afterthought
problems: A product that's functional but confusing to actually use
Inconsistent interfaces that slow teams down and erode trust
Features that get built but go unused because they're hard to find
items.example: An internal tool built feature-by-feature over several years, now inconsistent and slow to use, could be redesigned around a coherent design system without disrupting the workflows staff already rely on.

items.name: Systems Integration
items.tagline: Your existing tools, working as one
items.description: Connecting the software you already use — CRM, invoicing, payments, internal tools — into one coherent system instead of a set of disconnected accounts.
benefits: Data entered once, available everywhere it's needed
Fewer manual handoffs between disconnected tools
A single source of truth instead of three slightly different ones
problems: The same information re-entered manually into multiple systems
Tools that don't talk to each other, forcing manual reconciliation
No single accurate view because data lives in silos
items.example: A business re-entering the same customer details into its CRM, its invoicing tool and its support desk could have those systems connected so a single update flows everywhere automatically.

items.name: Technology Consulting
items.description: Technical advisory for organisations facing a decision they can't afford to get wrong — architecture direction, vendor evaluation, technical due diligence, or a second opinion before a major investment.
benefits: Independent judgement — no incentive to sell you a specific product
Grounded in delivery experience, not slide-deck theory
A clear, honest answer, including when the answer is 'don't build this yet'
problems: A major technical decision with no independent expert to sanity-check it
Uncertainty about whether an existing system can scale or needs replacing
Evaluating a vendor or agency proposal without in-house technical expertise
items.example: A leadership team about to commit significant budget to a platform rebuild could get an independent technical review first, to confirm the plan is sound before the spend is committed.

cta.kicker: Not sure which discipline you need
cta.heading: Tell us the problem. We'll tell you how we'd approach it.
cta.sub: Most projects touch more than one discipline — that's exactly why we work as one team.

hero.headline: How we think, shown in practice.
hero.sub: The pieces below are demonstration builds and concepts — created to show our approach and craft. They are not client work. As real projects ship, they'll be published here as case studies, clearly identified as such.

items.title: E-commerce storefront
items.desc: A demonstration of a full commerce platform — catalogue, cart, mobile-money and card checkout, and order management built for real operational use, not just a mockup.

items.title: SaaS analytics dashboard
items.desc: An example client workspace with live project tracking, revenue and activity — the kind of interface we build for SaaS clients running multi-tenant products.

items.title: Security operations platform
items.desc: A concept interface for a security monitoring workspace — threat detection, audit trails and compliance status, designed for how a SOC team actually works.

items.title: AI agents for business operations
items.desc: A concept showing an AI agent connected to a company's own data, handling support, sales enquiries or analysis with a human able to step in at any point.

items.title: Scalable cloud infrastructure
items.desc: An architecture concept for infrastructure built for high availability and progressive scale — the kind of foundation we design before writing a line of application code.

items.title: Mobile tracking application
items.desc: A demonstration mobile app with a statistics dashboard and recent activity feed — the pattern we use for field and operations apps.

cta.heading: Let's make your project our next real case study.

hero.headline: A technology company with an international standard.
hero.sub: NGUERA SENEGALENSIS TECH designs AI systems, software and digital infrastructure for organisations in the United Kingdom and worldwide — built to the same standard, wherever the client is.

origin.heading: Built in Senegal. Designed for the world.
origin.body: We were founded in Senegal, and that origin shapes how we work — resourceful, direct, and unwilling to cut corners just because a client is far away. But the standard we hold ourselves to isn't a regional one. We work remotely with clients across the United Kingdom, Europe, North America and the Middle East as a matter of course, not an exception.

mission.heading: Build the digital infrastructure our clients grow on.
mission.body: We don't deliver an isolated website or app and move on. We build technical and creative foundations meant to last — architecture, security and design decisions made properly the first time, so our clients aren't rebuilding in eighteen months.

approach.heading: The standards we don't compromise on.

points.title: Scope and timelines in writing
points.desc: Before work starts, you know what's being built, by when, and what happens if that changes — no ambiguity to hide behind later.

points.title: Security and architecture, not just features
points.desc: Authentication, data handling and system design are decided deliberately, not left as an afterthought once the interface looks finished.

points.title: Remote-first, timezone-aware delivery
points.desc: We work asynchronously and across time zones as standard practice, with clear communication cadence agreed upfront — distance isn't an excuse for silence.

points.title: Full handover, no lock-in
points.desc: You receive documented, clean code and full ownership of what we build — our value is the work itself, not making it hard to leave.

expertise.heading: The disciplines our team covers.
expertise.sub: Individual profiles — names, roles and photos — will be published here as our public team page grows. Below are the areas our team works in today.

areas.desc: AI agents, automation, applied machine learning

hero.headline: A clear starting point. A quote built around your project.
hero.sub: Every project is different — here's how we think about price, with figures to orient you.

pricing.note: Please read before anything else: the figures below are indicative starting points, not fixed prices. The final cost depends on exact scope, integrations (payments, AI, existing data) and your timeline. A precise quote follows a first conversation, with no obligation.

starter.name: Starter
starter.desc: A premium landing page or brochure site, ready to convert.

business.name: Business
business.desc: A full platform: e-commerce, SaaS, or a client workspace.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.desc: An AI platform, internal system, or custom multi-module product.

calculator.heading: Get an indicative estimate in under a minute.
calculator.disclaimer: Estimated budget — final pricing depends on scope. This is a starting point for conversation, not a quote.
calculator.cta: Turn this into a real quote

items.question: How is the final project price calculated?
items.answer: Based on the actual functional scope — number of pages, integrations, required security level — and your desired timeline. We avoid vague packages: every line of a quote is justified.

items.question: Is a deposit required to start?
items.answer: Yes, a deposit is typically required at signature, with the balance staged against project milestones. Exact terms are set out in each quote.

items.question: Do you offer support after launch?
items.answer: Yes, either as a monthly maintenance plan (fixes, security updates, small improvements) or one-off engagements depending on your needs.

items.question: Are local and regional payment methods supported?
items.answer: We integrate the payment methods relevant to your market technically — Stripe and cards for UK/international clients, or Wave and Orange Money for Senegal and West Africa. Opening the merchant account with each provider remains your responsibility — that's a regulatory requirement, not a technical choice.

cta.heading: Describe your project — we'll tell you exactly what it costs.

hero.headline: Let's talk about your project.
hero.sub: Fill in the form below or write to us directly — we reply within 48 working hours.

form.name: Full name
form.consent: I agree to be contacted about this enquiry. NGUERA SENEGALENSIS TECH will only use these details to respond — see our Privacy Policy.
form.marketingConsent: I'd also like to receive occasional updates about NGUERA SENEGALENSIS TECH (optional — you can unsubscribe anytime).
form.note: Your details are sent securely and used only to respond to this enquiry — see our Privacy Policy for how we handle your data.
form.successBody: Thank you — your request has reached our team. You'll receive a confirmation email shortly, and a reply within 48 working hours.
form.duplicateBody: We've already got a request from this email address in the last few minutes — no need to send it again. We'll be in touch within 48 working hours.
form.rateLimitedBody: You've submitted this form several times in a short period. Please wait a few minutes before trying again.
form.serverErrorBody: We couldn't save your request just now. Please try again in a moment, or email us directly.
form.validationErrorBanner: Please check the highlighted fields and try again.

info.emailNote: (placeholder — to be replaced with your address)
info.phoneNote: (placeholder — to be replaced with your number)
info.officeValue: Remote-first — serving the UK and worldwide
info.officeNote: (add a registered address here once available)

thankYou.headline: Thank you — we've got your request.
thankYou.body: A confirmation email is on its way, and our team will reply within 48 working hours with a clear proposal — no obligation on this first conversation.
